commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Draft maintainer PR needs author/owner follow-up for the SecretRef bounded-read gap and explicit compatibility acceptance before merge.; Needs attention: The diff is mostly hardening, but the remaining SecretRef provider-config read is a concrete auth-provider boundary gap.; Review finding: [P1] Bound SecretRef provider config reads — src/agents/model-auth.ts:125-126 (sha=d846e5a3d60a1cdce81ba3b09176ca52c899367f)
commands.review_summary:
The branch hardens many plugin, provider, Codex app-server, schema, metadata, tool descriptor, gateway, channel, outbound action, and SDK boundaries against malformed or unreadable synthetic inputs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the remaining review finding: source inspection shows an env SecretRef path still reads secrets.providers.<provider>.source and allowlist directly, so a throwing getter there would bypass the new bounded-reader pattern.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Draft maintainer PR needs author/owner follow-up for the SecretRef bounded-read gap and explicit compatibility acceptance before merge.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, diagnosed, audited, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, SDK helper APIs, realtime voice consult tool selection, MCP plugin-tool serving, embedded-agent tool-name allowlist collection, message-provider tool filtering, deferred follow-up tool description rewriting, Tool Search / Code Mode cataloging, Codex dynamic-tool filtering, message action validation, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, central runtime schema projection, embedded system-prompt rendering, system-prompt report construction, or outbound message action handling.
- Kept tests synthetic: current fixtures use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest slices guard Codex dynamic tool names, plugin tool descriptor cache capture, and older MCP/doctor fixture naming in the branch.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action metadata can crash, widen decisions, or bypass schema quarantine before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, approval forwarding, manifest/capability auth gating, local-model lean filtering, realtime voice consult tool selection, MCP plugin-tool serving, message-provider policy filtering, embedded-agent tool-name allowlist collection, deferred follow-up tool description rewriting, Tool Search / Code Mode cataloging, Codex dynamic-tool filtering, message action validation, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, central runtime schema projection, embedded system-prompt rendering, system-prompt report construction, or Codex app-server handling complete.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; earlier broad remote validation on direct AWS Crabbox Linux runners. Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable in this shell because `blacksmith auth login` is required.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Direct red/green focused Vitest repros for unreadable Codex dynamic-tool names in profile exclude filtering, memory-flush filtering, toolsAllow filtering, and inbound-vision image-tool filtering using synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` descriptors.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repros for plugin descriptor cache capture with unreadable names, unreadable parameters, non-JSON-safe parameters, and unreadable display summaries using synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` descriptors.
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-pr-update-final node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.vision-tools.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` on the touched plugin-cache, Codex dynamic-tool, and MCP/doctor fixture files.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` on the touched plugin-cache, Codex dynamic-tool, and MCP/doctor fixture files.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Current-tree scrub plus added-line scrub for reported plugin names, local paths, and private network literals.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- Earlier branch proofs include focused Vitest for the existing slices and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` gates: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_991a8c29acdf`, run `run_41811346ac17`, exit 0; and provider `aws`, lease `cbx_52b1fe398bd3`, run `run_c85914033aaa`, exit 0.

Evidence after fix:

- Final focused proof at rebased head passed 7 files / 97 tests.
- Latest branch head pushed: `d846e5a3d60a1cdce81ba3b09176ca52c899367f`.
- Latest local base after rebase: `origin/main` `70230f4235`.
- Plugin descriptor cache capture now skips uncapturable descriptors instead of throwing, while preserving healthy `mockplugin` descriptors and runtime tool registration.
- Codex dynamic-tool filtering omits unreadable names from profile excludes, memory-flush filtering, toolsAllow/wildcard filtering, sandbox shell selection, and inbound-vision image-tool filtering while preserving healthy readable siblings.
- Older branch fixtures have been scrubbed so the current tree uses synthetic `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*` names. Current-tree, added-line, and spec scrubs pass.
- Local autoreview was clean with no accepted/actionable findings after the plugin descriptor cache slice. The later fixture-name scrub is mechanical test-only replacement and was covered by focused tests plus static checks.

Observed result after fix:

- Hostile synthetic config/schema/provider/manifest/native-approval/message-action/plugin-tool inputs are bounded at the relevant owner boundary instead of throwing raw getter/enumeration/serialization/spread errors.
- Plugin descriptor cache capture returns no cache entry for unreadable names, unreadable parameters, or non-projectable schemas, and does not let one malformed descriptor poison the cache array.
- Codex inbound-vision filtering treats unreadable tool names as opaque non-image siblings, so it only removes the readable `image` tool when model vision and inbound images are both present.
- Tool Search / Code Mode cataloging omits unreadable names and malformed schemas, preserves healthy searchable siblings, validates catalog schemas with the central runtime schema projection before registration, and keeps parameterless client tools searchable with `{}` parameters.
- Existing focused slices continue to preserve healthy siblings while omitting only malformed descriptors across local-model lean filtering, embedded-agent allowlists, message-provider filtering, deferred follow-up descriptions, realtime voice consult tools, MCP plugin-tool serving, embedded system prompts, central schema projection, provider tools, system-prompt reports, and outbound message actions.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because `blacksmith` is not authenticated in this shell.
- The latest broad Crabbox gates predate the newest Tool Search / Code Mode, Codex dynamic-tool, and plugin descriptor cache slices; these slices have focused local proof, static proof, scrub proof, and clean local review where non-mechanical code changed.
- Full `src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` remains locally blocked by the linked worktree `quickjs-wasi` export issue noted in prior PR updates.
- GitHub PR checks are queued/running on the pushed head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new config surface. Existing malformed/unreadable config, manifest, provider, tool descriptor, diagnostic, catalog, dynamic-tool, or action inputs now fail closed or resolve as absent at bounded reads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed or unreadable tool/provider/plugin/native-approval/action metadata is rejected, omitted, or quarantined earlier instead of crashing runtime paths, bypassing schema quarantine, or broadening access.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict sanitation could omit a valid provider tool descriptor, plugin tool descriptor, Tool Search catalog entry, Codex dynamic tool, MCP plugin-tool descriptor, realtime custom voice tool, diagnostic payload, runtime projection descriptor, prompt-only tool name, message-provider-visible tool, deferred-followup-visible tool, embedded-agent allowlist-visible tool, or outbound action parameter.

How is that risk mitigated?

The changes preserve readable sibling entries, default only parameterless client catalog schemas to `{}`, fail closed only for explicit route/control/identity/idempotency-style surfaces, keep unreadable payload-only fields as absent, preserve duplicate readable tool counts in message-provider filtering, keep deferred follow-up unreadable names as non-matching opaque entries, and use focused regressions plus earlier broad AWS `check:changed` gates.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the draft PR at head `d846e5a3d60a1cdce81ba3b09176ca52c899367f`, or continue the next fuzzing slice from the current branch.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI is queued/running after the force-push. Blacksmith Testbox proof is blocked on local authentication. No local actionable autoreview findings remain.
target.author: vincentkoc
